diagrams.title: CUDA Thread Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchical organization of threads in CUDA from grid to individual thread
elements: Block (1D, 2D, or 3D collection of threads, max 1024)
Global ID: computed from block and thread IDs
diagrams.purpose: Understanding thread organization is fundamental to writing correct CUDA kernels and mapping problems to parallel threads

diagrams.title: GPU Memory Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Illustrates the different memory types in a GPU with their relative speeds and scopes
elements: Global Memory (VRAM): GB capacity, 400+ GB/s bandwidth
Shared Memory (L1): KB capacity, ~1.5 TB/s bandwidth
Local Memory: off-chip, spills from registers
diagrams.purpose: Memory access patterns significantly impact GPU performance. Understanding this hierarchy helps in optimizing data access for maximum bandwidth

diagrams.title: GPU Architecture Block Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the high-level architecture of a GPU with streaming multiprocessors and memory systems
elements: Each SM: Warp Scheduler, Execution Units, Register File, Shared Memory/L1
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how thousands of threads are scheduled across multiple SMs and how memory flows through the system
